title: Keetia inaequilatera
intro: Keetia inaequilatera, also known as Keetia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Keetia inaequilatera include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit significant pharmacological activities. In traditional medicine, Keetia is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make herbal teas, tonics, and ointments to treat various health conditions.
